summary: The 'Update Release Notes' agentic workflow has failed multiple times with the Copilot engine terminating unexpectedly — once during a git fetch of remote release branch refs, and once after completing successfully (exit code 0) but being reported as a failure by the harness.

bugSignals.errorMessage: Engine Failure: The `copilot` engine terminated unexpectedly

stepsToReproduce: Push a commit or tag to a release branch (e.g. v4.147.0-preview.2.1)
The 'Update Release Notes' agentic workflow triggers
The Copilot engine runs the release-notes skill
Engine terminates unexpectedly during git fetch of remote release branch refs, OR completes with exit code 0 but harness still reports failure
reproEvidence.environmentDetails: Branch: v4.147.0-preview.2.1; Workflow: update-release-notes; Harness: copilot-harness

repoLinks.description: Retry run 1 — engine failure during git fetch of release/* refs
Retry run 2 — agent completed exit code 0 but harness reported engine failure

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The issue recurs across multiple retry attempts with no code change in between, and a similar issue (#3800) was previously closed.

analysis.summary: The update-release-notes agentic workflow intermittently fails with Copilot engine crashes. One failure mode is during `git fetch origin 'refs/heads/release/*:refs/remotes/origin/release/*'` (network or shallow-clone issue). A second failure mode shows exit code 0 from the agent harness but the workflow is still flagged as an engine failure — possibly a post-processing or reporting issue in the agentic workflow framework.
analysis.rationale: Two distinct failure modes are visible in the comments: (1) engine crash during git fetch suggesting network timeouts or shallow-clone depth issues in the CI environment, and (2) a false-failure where exit code 0 was returned but the harness still filed an engine failure. Both point to reliability issues in the agentic workflow infrastructure rather than bugs in SkiaSharp's release notes generation logic itself. This is classified as type/bug in area/Build because it is a recurring, reproducible failure in the CI automation.

keySignals.interpretation: Engine terminated during a shallow fetch of release branch refs — likely a network timeout or depth limit issue in the CI environment.

keySignals.text: [copilot-harness] success on attempt 1: totalDuration=9m 54s ... done: exitCode=0 ... Engine Failure: The `copilot` engine terminated unexpectedly
keySignals.interpretation: Agent completed with exit code 0, but the harness still classified the run as an engine failure — suggests a post-processing or safe-outputs reporting issue.
The auto-created issue has an expiry, indicating it is generated by the agentic workflows infrastructure and was expected to be resolved and closed before expiry.

codeInvestigation.finding: Step 2 runs `git fetch origin main --quiet` and `git checkout -B main origin/main`, but earlier the workflow also fetches `refs/heads/release/*:refs/remotes/origin/release/*` with `--depth=1` (visible in the engine failure output). Shallow fetch of many release branches can time out or fail in restricted network environments.
Lock file exists alongside the workflow definition, consistent with an agentic workflow setup.

nextQuestions: Is the git fetch of release/* refs happening inside the release-notes skill script, and if so, is it necessary?
Why did run 26366435880 report engine failure despite exit code 0 — is there a safe-outputs reporting step missing?
Has #3800 been investigated and closed for the same root cause? What was the resolution?

actionability.reason: Recurring engine failure in agentic workflow with two distinct failure modes. Needs deeper investigation into whether git fetch depth/network constraints cause the first failure, and why a successful exit-code-0 run still triggers an engine-failure report.

actions.description: Cross-reference previously closed duplicate workflow failure issue
Post analysis noting the two failure modes and suggesting investigation directions
actions.comment:
This issue captures two distinct failure modes in the `update-release-notes` agentic workflow:

1. **Engine crash during git fetch** (run [26178491306](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/actions/runs/26178491306)): The Copilot engine terminated during `git fetch origin 'refs/heads/release/*:refs/remotes/origin/release/*' --depth=1`. This may be a shallow-clone timeout or network constraint in the runner environment.

2. **False failure on exit 0** (run [26366435880](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/actions/runs/26366435880)): The agent completed successfully with `exitCode=0` and `totalDuration=9m 54s`, but the harness still flagged it as an engine failure. This suggests a missing or failed safe-outputs step at the end of the workflow run.

A similar issue was previously opened as #3800. Recommended next steps:
- Check if the release-notes skill script fetches all release/* refs unnecessarily and whether that fetch can be constrained or skipped
- Inspect run [26366435880](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/actions/runs/26366435880/agentic_workflow) for any missing safe-outputs call that would signal successful completion to the harness
